cards.question: Applying the rule of Dirichlet‚Äôs Principle it is possible to prove that among five positive integers there are x such integers whose sum can be divided by 3, if

With 5 numbers in 3 residue classes, by pigeonhole some class has ‚Č•2 elements. If class 0 has ‚Č•2: any pair sums to 0 mod 3. Otherwise classes 1 and 2 each have ‚Č•2 and there's at least one from class 0 ‚ÄĒ pick one from each ‚úď. So always ‚ąÉ subset of size 2 or 3 with sum div by 3.
